description_long: This object is a large, robust knife with a straight, single-edged blade that features a pronounced angular tip, reminiscent of a cleaver or machete. The blade has a small circular hole near the spine and three shallow grooves or indentations on the lower portion, likely for aesthetic or functional grip. The handle is straight and blocky, with a rectangular cross-section and a squared-off pommel, suggesting a utilitarian design. A prominent rectangular guard separates the blade from the handle, providing protection for the user's hand. The entire object appears to be a single dark color, possibly matte black, giving it a tactical or industrial appearance. There are no visible decorative elements or embellishments, emphasizing function over form.
description: A large, angular, single-edged knife with a straight, blocky handle and a prominent rectangular guard, all in a matte black finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the blade, including the angular tip, circular hole, grooves, and the straight handle with a rectangular guard.
description_view_1: Edge-on view displaying the thinness of the blade and handle, with the pointed tip and rectangular guard visible in profile.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, showing the blade's profile, grooves, and handle details.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, again highlighting the blade's thinness and pointed tip.
attribute_handle_design: straight, rectangular, blocky, matte finish, no visible grip texture
